title: Container Threat Models, Users, Capabilities, Seccomp, Rootless, and Daemon Authority · Deployment Triage
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective least-privilege container threat model and hardening profile; isolate the first causal fault in container threat models, users, capabilities, seccomp, rootless, and daemon authority and preserve a regression check before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in least-privilege container threat model and hardening profile and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through predictio…
objectives: Model untrusted image, build, registry, daemon, runtime, kernel, mount, device, network, and supply-chain threats without treating containers as sandboxes by default.
Run as a deliberate non-root UID/GID and reconcile image ownership, mounted files, rootless mappings, and required privileged startup work.
Start from dropped capabilities and add only measured needs while treating privileged, devices, host namespaces, and Docker socket access as host authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Threat Boundary Host Kernel
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug document parser runs privileged as root, mounts daemon authority, writes the image filesystem, has network access, and no resource caps. Remove authority and state the remaining shared-kernel risk. Competency probe: Model untrusted image, build, registry, daemon, runtime, kernel, mount, device, network, and supply-chain threats without treating containers as sandboxes by default.

Inspect the Docker Threat Boundary Host Kernel trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Rootless Userns Daemon Socket
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-rootless-userns-daemon-socket-idl1w0". Tailor its security-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Rootless Userns Daemon Socket.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Rootless Userns Daemon Socket into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Rootless Userns Daemon Socket, keep Docker Rootless Userns Daemon Socket connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-rootless-userns-daemon-socket-idl1w0
  - competency: docker-rootless-userns-daemon-socket
    artifact: security-policy.yaml
    decision: rootless userns daemon socket
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: rootless remote builder on an arm64 staging host
    fault-injection: invalidate one cache input without changing application source
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: idl1w0
    change: |
      user: 10001:10001
      read_only: true
      cap_drop: [ALL]
      security_opt: [no-new-privileges:true]
      tmpfs: [/tmp]
      network_mode: none
    verify: docker inspect document-worker --format json
    failure: Reject privileged mode, daemon-socket mounting, broad devices, root-by-default, or a claim that containers form a hostile-code sandbox.
    repair: Remove daemon authority, choose rootless or user namespaces where applicable, drop capabilities, bound writable paths, and state residual kernel risk.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Compare rootless mode, userns remap, rootful daemon groups, contexts, remote TLS, and socket proxies and document remaining authority.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug Docker Rootless Userns Daemon Socket, name the daemon authority, immutable identity, process, storage, network, secret, and cleanup boundary, Docker Rootless Userns Daemon Socket precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug Docker Rootless Userns Daemon Socket, trace this concrete input and predict build, image, container, mount, network, Compose, policy, and teardown evidence before reviewing it: A debug document parser runs privileged as root, mounts daemon authority, writes the image filesystem, has network access, and no resource caps. Remove authority and state the remaining shared-kernel risk. Competency probe: Compare rootless mode, userns remap, rootful daemon groups, contexts, remote TLS, and socket proxies and document remaining authority.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Docker Rootless Userns Daemon Socket, challenge the documented misconception with manifest, inspect, lifecycle, failure, repair, and transfer evidence: Membership in the docker group grants ordinary non-administrative access only.
